## Button

`import { Button, buttonVariants } from '@btcv/ui/Button'`

Primary action button with multiple visual variants and sizes. Uses children composition for icons -- place SVG icons directly as children. Can render as a Radix Slot via `asChild`.

Icon + text spacing is built in: the button has a `gap-2` between children, so **do not add spacing utilities** (`mr-2`, `gap-2`, `space-x-*`) around the icon -- just drop the icon in as a child.

### Button Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| variant | `"primary"` \| `"secondary"` \| `"outline"` \| `"destructive"` \| `"success"` \| `"warning"` \| `"info"` \| `"ghost"` \| `"ghost-primary"` \| `"ghost-destructive"` \| `"ghost-success"` \| `"ghost-warning"` \| `"ghost-info"` \| `"semi-primary"` \| `"semi-destructive"` \| `"semi-success"` \| `"semi-warning"` \| `"semi-info"` \| `"link"` | `"primary"` | Visual style variant. `ghost-xxx`: transparent bg, colored text, colored bg on hover. `semi-xxx`: colored bg (15% opacity), darker on hover. |
| size | `"sm"` \| `"md"` \| `"lg"` \| `"icon"` | `"md"` | Button size. `"icon"` renders a 36x36 square. |
| loading | `boolean` | `false` | Shows a spinning loader and disables the button. Children are hidden while loading. |
| asChild | `boolean` | `false` | Render as a child Slot element instead of a native button. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<button>` HTML attributes.

### buttonVariants

CVA variant generator. Returns a Tailwind class string for a given variant/size combination.

```jsx
const cls = buttonVariants({ variant: "outline", size: "lg" });
```

### Example

```jsx
<Button variant="primary" size="md">Save changes</Button>

<Button variant="primary"><Plus /> Add Game</Button>

<Button variant="ghost" size="icon"><Settings /></Button>

<Button loading>Saving...</Button>
```

---

## Card

`import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, CardFooter } from '@btcv/ui/Card'`

Rounded container with border, glassmorphism background, and optional accent border.

### Card Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| accentSide | `"top"` \| `"bottom"` \| `"left"` \| `"right"` | -- | Side of the card to display the accent border. |
| accentColor | `"primary"` \| `"success"` \| `"info"` \| `"warning"` \| `"destructive"` | -- | Color of the accent border. Both `accentSide` and `accentColor` must be set for the accent to appear. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<div>` HTML attributes. Forwards ref.

### CardHeader

Flex column container with `space-y-1.5` gap. Accepts `className` and all `<div>` attributes. Forwards ref.

### CardTitle

Text element with `text-base font-semibold`. Accepts `className` and all `<div>` attributes. Forwards ref.

### CardDescription

Muted text element (`text-sm text-muted-foreground`). Accepts `className` and all `<div>` attributes. Forwards ref.

### CardContent

Plain div wrapper. Accepts `className` and all `<div>` attributes. Forwards ref.

### CardFooter

Flex row with `items-center gap-2`. Accepts `className` and all `<div>` attributes. Forwards ref.

### Example

```jsx
<Card accentSide="top" accentColor="primary">
  <CardHeader>
    <CardTitle>Server Status</CardTitle>
    <CardDescription>Real-time monitoring</CardDescription>
  </CardHeader>
  <CardContent>
    <p>All systems operational.</p>
  </CardContent>
  <CardFooter>
    <Button size="sm">Refresh</Button>
  </CardFooter>
</Card>
```

## Badge

`import { Badge, badgeVariants, StatusDot } from '@btcv/ui/Badge'`

### Badge

Inline label badge with semantic color variants and multiple sizes.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| variant | `"default"` \| `"success"` \| `"destructive"` \| `"warning"` \| `"info"` \| `"secondary"` \| `"outline"` | `"default"` | Color variant controlling background, text, and border. |
| size | `"sm"` \| `"md"` \| `"lg"` | `"md"` | Badge size controlling font size and padding. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<span>` HTML attributes.

### badgeVariants

CVA variant generator. Returns a Tailwind class string for a given variant/size combination.

```jsx
const cls = badgeVariants({ variant: "success", size: "sm" });
```

### StatusDot

Small colored dot indicator for connection or activity status, with an optional label.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| status | `"online"` \| `"offline"` \| `"loading"` \| `"idle"` | `"offline"` | Current status controlling the dot color. |
| label | `ReactNode` | -- | Optional text label displayed next to the dot. |
| pulse | `boolean` | `false` | Whether the dot should pulse (animate). |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### Example

```jsx
<Badge variant="success" size="sm">Active</Badge>

<StatusDot status="online" label="Connected" pulse />
```

---

## Alert

`import { Alert } from '@btcv/ui/Alert'`

Alert banner for displaying contextual messages with icon, variant, title, and description. Built on Card with a left accent border.

### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>` | -- | Icon displayed at the start of the alert. |
| variant | `"primary"` \| `"info"` \| `"success"` \| `"destructive"` \| `"warning"` | -- | Accent color variant controlling the left border and icon color. |
| title | `ReactNode` | -- | Title displayed prominently above the description. |
| children | `ReactNode` | -- | Description text content. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<div>` HTML attributes (except `title`).

### Example

```jsx
import { AlertTriangle } from 'lucide-react';

<Alert icon={AlertTriangle} variant="warning" title="Attention">
  Votre certificat SSL expire dans 43 jours.
</Alert>
```

## Input

`import { Label, Input, Select, SelectItem, SelectGroup, Toggle, Checkbox, RadioGroup, RadioGroupItem, Slider, Textarea } from '@btcv/ui/Input'`

### Label

Styled form label with uppercase tracking and muted foreground color. Built on Radix UI Label primitive.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<label>` HTML attributes.

### Input

Text input field with optional label, icon, prefix/suffix, and error message. When `type="password"`, an eye toggle is automatically added.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| label | `string` | -- | Optional label text displayed above the input. |
| error | `ReactNode` | -- | Error message displayed below the input. When set, the input border turns red. |
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>` | -- | Icon component rendered inside the input. |
| iconPosition | `"left"` \| `"right"` | `"left"` | Side of the input where the icon is rendered. |
| prefix | `ReactNode` | -- | ReactNode rendered inside the input on the left (e.g. currency symbol). |
| suffix | `ReactNode` | -- | ReactNode rendered inside the input on the right (e.g. unit label). |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the inner `<input>` element. |

Also accepts all native `<input>` HTML attributes (except `prefix` and `suffix`).

### Select

Select dropdown built on Radix UI Select. Use `SelectItem` for options and `SelectGroup` for grouped options. Set `native` for a plain `<select>` element.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| label | `string` | -- | Optional label text displayed above the select. |
| placeholder | `string` | -- | Placeholder text shown when no value is selected. |
| value | `string` | -- | Controlled value. |
| defaultValue | `string` | -- | Default value for uncontrolled usage. |
| onValueChange | `(value: string) => void` | -- | Callback fired when the selected value changes. |
| disabled | `boolean` | -- | Whether the select is disabled. |
| native | `boolean` | `false` | Render a native `<select>` element instead of Radix UI. When true, children should be `<option>` and `<optgroup>` elements. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the trigger. |
| children | `ReactNode` | -- | SelectItem/SelectGroup children (Radix mode) or option/optgroup children (native mode). |

### SelectItem

Individual option inside a Select dropdown.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| value | `string` | **(required)** | The value submitted when this item is selected. |
| disabled | `boolean` | -- | Whether this item is disabled. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |
| children | `ReactNode` | -- | Display content. |

### SelectGroup

Group of SelectItem options with an optional label.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| label | `string` | -- | Optional group label displayed above the items. |
| children | `ReactNode` | -- | SelectItem children. |

### Toggle

Toggle switch built on Radix UI Switch primitive with optional label and description.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| label | `string` | -- | Optional label text displayed next to the switch. |
| description | `string` | -- | Optional description text displayed below the label. |
| checked | `boolean` | -- | Controlled checked state. |
| onChange | `(checked: boolean) => void` | -- | Callback fired when the toggle state changes. |
| disabled | `boolean` | -- | Whether the toggle is disabled. |
| labelPosition | `"left"` \| `"right"` | `"right"` | Side where the label is rendered. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### Checkbox

Checkbox built on Radix UI Checkbox primitive with optional label and description.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| label | `string` | -- | Optional label text displayed next to the checkbox. |
| description | `string` | -- | Optional description text displayed below the label. |
| checked | `boolean` | -- | Controlled checked state. |
| onChange | `(checked: boolean) => void` | -- | Callback fired when the checkbox state changes. |
| disabled | `boolean` | -- | Whether the checkbox is disabled. |
| labelPosition | `"left"` \| `"right"` | `"right"` | Side where the label is rendered. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### RadioGroup

Radio group built on Radix UI RadioGroup primitive. Use with RadioGroupItem children.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| label | `string` | -- | Optional label text displayed above the radio group. |
| value | `string` | -- | Controlled value of the selected radio item. |
| onValueChange | `(value: string) => void` | -- | Callback fired when the selected value changes. |
| disabled | `boolean` | -- | Whether the entire group is disabled. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |
| children | `ReactNode` | -- | RadioGroupItem children. |

### RadioGroupItem

Individual radio option inside a RadioGroup.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| value | `string` | **(required)** | The value submitted when this item is selected. |
| label | `string` | -- | Label text displayed next to the radio. |
| description | `string` | -- | Optional description text displayed below the label. |
| disabled | `boolean` | -- | Whether this item is disabled. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### Slider

Slider built on Radix UI Slider primitive with optional label and value display.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| label | `string` | -- | Optional label text displayed above the slider. |
| value | `number[]` | -- | Controlled value. Array for single or range slider. |
| onValueChange | `(value: number[]) => void` | -- | Callback fired when the value changes. |
| min | `number` | `0` | Minimum value. |
| max | `number` | `100` | Maximum value. |
| step | `number` | `1` | Step increment. |
| disabled | `boolean` | -- | Whether the slider is disabled. |
| renderValue | `(value: number) => string` | `String` | Format function for displayed values. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### Textarea

Resizable textarea with optional label, icon, prefix/suffix, and error message.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| label | `string` | -- | Optional label text displayed above the textarea. |
| error | `ReactNode` | -- | Error message displayed below the textarea. When set, the border turns red. |
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>` | -- | Icon component rendered inside the textarea container. |
| iconPosition | `"left"` \| `"right"` | `"left"` | Side of the textarea where the icon is rendered. |
| prefix | `ReactNode` | -- | ReactNode rendered inside the container on the left. |
| suffix | `ReactNode` | -- | ReactNode rendered inside the container on the right. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<textarea>` HTML attributes (except `prefix`).

### Example

```jsx
<Input label="Email" placeholder="you@example.com" icon={Mail} />

<Select label="Instance Type" placeholder="Selectionner...">
  <SelectItem value="docker">Docker</SelectItem>
  <SelectItem value="totanus">Totanus</SelectItem>
</Select>

<Toggle label="Dark mode" checked={isDark} onChange={setIsDark} />

<Checkbox label="Accept terms" checked={accepted} onChange={setAccepted} />

<RadioGroup label="Plan" value={plan} onValueChange={setPlan}>
  <RadioGroupItem value="free" label="Free" />
  <RadioGroupItem value="pro" label="Pro" description="All features included" />
</RadioGroup>

<Slider label="Volume" value={[75]} onValueChange={setVolume} />

<Textarea label="Description" placeholder="Enter details..." />
```

## EasyForm

`import { EasyForm } from '@btcv/ui/EasyForm'`

Declarative form builder. Define fields as a JSON array and EasyForm renders the appropriate Input components with built-in validation. Forwards ref to the `<form>` element.

### EasyForm Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| fields | `EasyFormField[]` | **(required)** | Array of field configurations. |
| onSubmit | `(values: FormValues) => void` | -- | Called with all form values when submitted and validation passes. |
| onChange | `(values: FormValues, changedField: string) => void` | -- | Called whenever a field value changes. |
| defaultValues | `FormValues` | -- | Default values for the form fields. Keys are field names. |
| submitButton | `ReactNode` | -- | Custom submit button. If not provided, no submit button is rendered. |
| resetButton | `ReactNode` | -- | Custom reset button. Resets all fields to defaultValues. |
| gap | `string` | `"gap-4"` | Tailwind gap class between fields. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the form element. |

`FormValues` is `Record<string, unknown>`.

### Field Types

Each field in the `fields` array has a `type` that determines the rendered component. All field types share these base properties:

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| name | `string` | Unique field name, used as key in values. |
| label | `string` | Label displayed above/next to the field. |
| placeholder | `string` | Placeholder text. |
| disabled | `boolean` | Whether the field is disabled. |
| validation | `ValidationRules` | Validation rules (see below). |

**TextField** (`type: "text" | "email" | "password" | "number"`): Additional props: `icon`, `iconPosition`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `defaultValue`.

**SelectField** (`type: "select"`): Additional props: `options: (SelectOption | SelectOptionGroup)[]`, `native`, `defaultValue`. Each `SelectOption` has `{ value, label, disabled? }`. Each `SelectOptionGroup` has `{ group, options: SelectOption[] }`.

**TextareaField** (`type: "textarea"`): Additional props: `rows`, `icon`, `iconPosition`, `defaultValue`.

**ToggleField** (`type: "toggle"`): Additional props: `description`, `labelPosition`, `defaultValue` (boolean).

**CheckboxField** (`type: "checkbox"`): Additional props: `description`, `labelPosition`, `defaultValue` (boolean).

**RadioField** (`type: "radio"`): Additional props: `options: RadioOption[]` (each has `{ value, label, description?, disabled? }`), `defaultValue`.

**SliderField** (`type: "slider"`): Additional props: `min`, `max`, `step`, `renderValue`, `defaultValue` (number[]).

**RowField** (`type: "row"`): Contains `fields: EasyFormField[]` to display multiple fields side by side in a grid row.

### ValidationRules

| Rule | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| required | `boolean \| string` | Field is required. Pass a string for a custom error message. |
| min | `number \| { value: number; message: string }` | Minimum value (number/slider) or minimum length (string). |
| max | `number \| { value: number; message: string }` | Maximum value (number/slider) or maximum length (string). |
| minLength | `number \| { value: number; message: string }` | Minimum string length. |
| maxLength | `number \| { value: number; message: string }` | Maximum string length. |
| pattern | `RegExp \| { value: RegExp; message: string }` | Regex pattern the value must match. |
| custom | `(value: unknown, values: FormValues) => string \| undefined` | Custom validator. Return an error message string, or undefined if valid. |

### Example

```jsx
<EasyForm
  fields={[
    { name: "email", type: "email", label: "Email", validation: { required: true } },
    { name: "password", type: "password", label: "Password", validation: { minLength: 8 } },
    {
      type: "row",
      fields: [
        { name: "first", type: "text", label: "First Name" },
        { name: "last", type: "text", label: "Last Name" },
      ],
    },
    {
      name: "role", type: "select", label: "Role",
      options: [{ value: "admin", label: "Admin" }, { value: "user", label: "User" }],
    },
    { name: "terms", type: "checkbox", label: "I accept the terms" },
  ]}
  onSubmit={(values) => console.log(values)}
  submitButton={<Button>Submit</Button>}
/>
```

## Table

`import { Table, TableHeader, TableBody, TableRow, TableHead, TableCell, TableCaption, EasyTable } from '@btcv/ui/Table'`

### Primitive Components

Shadcn-style table primitives. Each accepts `className` and forwards all native HTML attributes for their respective elements.

| Component | Renders | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| Table | `<table>` wrapped in scrollable `<div>` | Scrollable table wrapper with rounded border. |
| TableHeader | `<thead>` | Container for table header rows. |
| TableBody | `<tbody>` | Container for table body rows. |
| TableRow | `<tr>` | Table row with hover highlight (`bg-primary/8`). |
| TableHead | `<th>` | Header cell. Uppercase, muted foreground, bg-muted. |
| TableCell | `<td>` | Data cell. |
| TableCaption | `<caption>` | Table caption. |

### EasyTable

Declarative table with columns + data API. Built on the primitives above.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| columns | `EasyTableColumn<T>[]` | **(required)** | Array of column definitions. |
| data | `T[]` | **(required)** | Array of row data objects. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

**EasyTableColumn** shape:

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| key | `string` | Property key used to access the cell value from each row object. |
| label | `ReactNode` | Header label. |
| width | `string` | Optional fixed column width (CSS value, e.g. `"200px"`). |
| render | `(value: unknown, row: T) => ReactNode` | Custom render function for cell content. |

### Example

```jsx
{/* Primitive usage */}
<Table>
  <TableHeader>
    <TableRow>
      <TableHead>Name</TableHead>
      <TableHead>Status</TableHead>
    </TableRow>
  </TableHeader>
  <TableBody>
    <TableRow>
      <TableCell>Alice</TableCell>
      <TableCell><Badge>Active</Badge></TableCell>
    </TableRow>
  </TableBody>
</Table>

{/* Declarative usage */}
<EasyTable
  columns={[
    { key: "name", label: "Name" },
    { key: "status", label: "Status", render: (v) => <Badge>{v}</Badge> },
  ]}
  data={[{ name: "Alice", status: "Active" }]}
/>
```

---

## Tabs

`import { Tabs, NavPills } from '@btcv/ui/Tabs'`

### Tabs

Underline-style tab navigation built on Radix UI Tabs. The active tab is highlighted with a primary-colored bottom border.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| tabs | `TabItem[]` | **(required)** | Array of tab definitions. |
| active | `string` | **(required)** | Key of the currently active tab. |
| onChange | `(key: string) => void` | **(required)** | Callback fired when a tab is selected. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the tab list container. |

**TabItem** shape: `{ key: string; label: ReactNode; icon?: ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }> }`

### NavPills

Pill-style navigation with rounded active indicator. Alternative to Tabs for horizontal navigation.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| items | `NavPillItem[]` | **(required)** | Array of pill item definitions. |
| active | `string` | **(required)** | Key of the currently active pill. |
| onChange | `(key: string) => void` | **(required)** | Callback fired when a pill is clicked. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the nav container. |

**NavPillItem** shape: `{ key: string; label: ReactNode; icon?: ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }> }`

### Example

```jsx
<Tabs
  tabs={[
    { key: "overview", label: "Overview", icon: LayoutDashboard },
    { key: "settings", label: "Settings", icon: Settings },
  ]}
  active={tab}
  onChange={setTab}
/>

<NavPills
  items={[
    { key: "all", label: "All" },
    { key: "active", label: "Active" },
  ]}
  active={filter}
  onChange={setFilter}
/>
```

---

## Dialog

`import { Dialog, DialogTrigger, DialogClose, DialogPortal, DialogOverlay, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogFooter, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogBody, alertDialog, confirmDialog, promptDialog } from '@btcv/ui/Dialog'`

### Declarative Components

Built on Radix UI Dialog. Use these for custom dialog layouts.

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Dialog | Root component. Radix `Dialog.Root`. Props: `open`, `onOpenChange`. |
| DialogTrigger | Radix `Dialog.Trigger`. Wraps the element that opens the dialog. |
| DialogClose | Radix `Dialog.Close`. Wraps the element that closes the dialog. |
| DialogPortal | Radix `Dialog.Portal`. |
| DialogOverlay | Backdrop overlay with blur and fade animation. Accepts `className`. |
| DialogContent | Centered content panel with glassmorphism styling and animations. Max width 480px. |
| DialogHeader | Flex column for title/description. Padded with `px-5`. |
| DialogBody | Body section. Padded with `px-5`. |
| DialogFooter | Footer with right-aligned buttons. Padded, with top border. |
| DialogTitle | Styled title (base size, semibold). |
| DialogDescription | Styled description (small, muted). |

**DialogContent** extra props:

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| hideClose | `boolean` | `false` | Hide the default close (X) button. |

### alertDialog (function)

Imperative alert dialog with a single button. Returns a `Promise<void>` that resolves when the user dismisses the dialog.

**Parameters** (`AlertDialogOptions`):

| Param | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| title | `ReactNode` | **(required)** | Dialog title. |
| description | `ReactNode` | -- | Short description below the title. |
| message | `ReactNode` | -- | Body message. |
| button | `ReactNode` | `"OK"` | Custom button content. |

```jsx
await alertDialog({
  title: "Session expiree",
  message: "Veuillez vous reconnecter.",
});
```

### confirmDialog (function)

Imperative confirmation dialog. Returns a `Promise<boolean>` -- `true` if confirmed, `false` if cancelled.

**Parameters** (`ConfirmDialogOptions`):

| Param | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| title | `ReactNode` | **(required)** | Dialog title. |
| description | `ReactNode` | -- | Short description below the title. |
| message | `ReactNode` | -- | Body message between header and footer. |
| confirmButton | `ReactNode` | `"Confirmer"` | Custom confirm button. Pass a string or a `<Button>` element. |
| cancelButton | `ReactNode` | `"Annuler"` | Custom cancel button. Pass a string or a `<Button>` element. |
| closable | `boolean` | `true` | Allow closing via overlay click, Escape, or X button. |
| onSuccess | `() => Promise<void> \| void` | -- | Async callback executed on confirm. Dialog stays open while pending. |
| onCancel | `() => Promise<void> \| void` | -- | Async callback executed on cancel. Dialog stays open while pending. |

```jsx
const ok = await confirmDialog({ title: "Delete?" });
if (ok) deleteItem();
```

### promptDialog (function)

Imperative prompt dialog with an EasyForm. Returns a `Promise<FormValues | null>` -- form values on confirm, `null` on cancel. The confirm button triggers EasyForm validation; the dialog only closes when validation passes and the optional `onSuccess` promise resolves.

**Parameters** (`PromptDialogOptions`):

| Param | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| title | `ReactNode` | **(required)** | Dialog title. |
| description | `ReactNode` | -- | Short description below the title. |
| message | `ReactNode` | -- | Body message displayed above the form fields. |
| fields | `EasyFormField[]` | **(required)** | EasyForm field configurations. |
| defaultValues | `FormValues` | -- | Default values for the form fields. |
| confirmButton | `ReactNode` | `"Confirmer"` | Custom confirm button. |
| cancelButton | `ReactNode` | `"Annuler"` | Custom cancel button. |
| closable | `boolean` | `true` | Allow closing via overlay/Escape/X. |
| onSuccess | `(values: FormValues) => Promise<void> \| void` | -- | Async callback executed on confirm with form values. Dialog stays open while pending. |
| onCancel | `() => Promise<void> \| void` | -- | Async callback executed on cancel. |

```jsx
const values = await promptDialog({
  title: "Creer un serveur",
  fields: [
    { name: "name", type: "text", label: "Nom", validation: { required: true } },
    { name: "slots", type: "number", label: "Slots", defaultValue: "20" },
  ],
  onSuccess: (v) => api.createServer(v),
});
if (values) console.log("Created:", values);
```

---

## Toast

`import { ToastProvider, toast } from '@btcv/ui/Toast'`

### ToastProvider

Toast notification container built on Sonner. Mount once at the app root.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| position | `"top-left"` \| `"top-right"` \| `"bottom-left"` \| `"bottom-right"` \| `"top-center"` \| `"bottom-center"` | `"bottom-right"` | Screen position for toast notifications. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### toast (object)

Pre-styled toast trigger functions. Each method shows a toast with the appropriate icon and left border color.

| Method | Signature | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| toast.success | `(msgOrOptions: ToastInput) => void` | Green success toast. |
| toast.error | `(msgOrOptions: ToastInput) => void` | Red error toast. |
| toast.warning | `(msgOrOptions: ToastInput) => void` | Yellow warning toast. |
| toast.info | `(msgOrOptions: ToastInput) => void` | Blue info toast. |

`ToastInput` is `ReactNode | ToastOptions`.

**ToastOptions** shape:

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| message | `ReactNode` | **(required)** | Toast body text. |
| title | `ReactNode` | -- | Optional bold title displayed above the message. |
| icon | `ReactNode` | -- | Override the default variant icon. |
| duration | `number` | `4000` | Auto-dismiss delay in milliseconds. |

### Example

```jsx
// Mount provider once
<ToastProvider position="bottom-right" />

// Simple string
toast.success("Changes saved");
toast.error("Something went wrong");

// Object with title
toast.success({ title: "Build #42", message: "Completed in 1m32s" });

// Object with custom icon
toast.info({ message: "Syncing...", icon: <Loader2 className="animate-spin" /> });
```

---

## Navbar

`import { Navbar } from '@btcv/ui/Navbar'`

Sticky top navigation bar with glassmorphism styling. Children are centered absolutely for navigation links; actions are right-aligned.

### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| appName | `ReactNode` | -- | Application name displayed in the left branding area. |
| appSuffix | `ReactNode` | -- | Colored suffix appended to the app name (rendered in primary color). |
| logo | `ReactNode \| false` | Primary circle | Logo element. Pass a ReactNode to replace, or `false` to hide. |
| actions | `ReactNode` | -- | Action elements (buttons, icons) rendered on the right side. |
| children | `ReactNode` | -- | Navigation content centered in the navbar (hidden on mobile). |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<header>` HTML attributes.

### Example

```jsx
<Navbar appName="BATCAVE" appSuffix="UI" actions={<Button size="sm">Login</Button>}>
  <NavPills items={navItems} active={page} onChange={setPage} />
</Navbar>

{/* Custom logo */}
<Navbar logo={<img src="/logo.svg" className="w-7 h-7" />} appName="BATCAVE" />

{/* No logo */}
<Navbar logo={false} appName="BATCAVE" />
```

## ProgressBar

`import { ProgressBar } from '@btcv/ui/ProgressBar'`

Animated progress bar built on Radix UI Progress with color variants and an optional label.

### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| value | `number` | `0` | Current progress value (0-100). Clamped automatically. |
| variant | `"gold"` \| `"success"` \| `"destructive"` \| `"info"` | `"gold"` | Color variant for the filled portion. |
| size | `"sm"` \| `"md"` \| `"lg"` | `"md"` | Track height. |
| indeterminate | `boolean` | `false` | Shows a full-width bar with a shimmer animation. Useful when the progress value is unknown. |
| label | `ReactNode` | -- | Optional label displayed above the bar with a percentage indicator. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### Example

```jsx
<ProgressBar value={75} variant="success" size="md" label="Upload progress" />

<ProgressBar indeterminate variant="gold" label="Chargement..." />
```

---

## CodeBlock

`import { CodeBlock, CopyButton, LogLine, LogViewer } from '@btcv/ui/CodeBlock'`

### CodeBlock

Syntax-highlighted code block with line numbers, copy button, diff markers, and line highlighting. Supports JS/TS/JSX/TSX, CSS, HTML, JSON, Bash/Shell, and Python. Automatically detects diff mode when lines start with `+` or `-`.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| children | `string` | **(required)** | Source code string to display. Trailing newline is stripped automatically. |
| filename | `string` | -- | Filename shown in the header bar. Takes precedence over language label. |
| language | `string` | -- | Language identifier for syntax highlighting (e.g. `"js"`, `"python"`, `"css"`, `"bash"`, `"json"`). |
| lineNumbers | `boolean` | `true` | Whether to display line numbers. |
| highlightLines | `number[]` | `[]` | Array of 1-based line numbers to visually highlight. |
| showCopyButton | `boolean` | `true` | Whether to show the copy-to-clipboard button in the header. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the outer container. |
| aria-label | `string` | filename or `"Code block"` | Accessible label for the code region. |

### CopyButton

Small icon button that copies text to the clipboard and shows a check icon on success.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| text | `string` | **(required)** | Text content to copy to the clipboard. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### LogLine

Single log entry line with a colored level badge and optional timestamp. Designed to be stacked vertically.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| level | `"info"` \| `"warn"` \| `"error"` \| `"debug"` | `"info"` | Severity level controlling the badge color. |
| time | `string` | -- | Timestamp string displayed after the level badge. |
| children | `ReactNode` | -- | Log message content. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### Example

```jsx
<CodeBlock language="js" filename="index.js" highlightLines={[3]}>
{`const x = 1;
const y = 2;
const sum = x + y;`}
</CodeBlock>

<CopyButton text="npm install @btcv/ui" />

<LogLine level="error" time="12:34:56">Connection refused</LogLine>
```

### LogViewer

Log viewer with level filtering, auto-scroll, and Card styling.

#### LogViewer Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| logs | `LogEntry[]` | -- | Array of log entries to display. |
| maxHeight | `string \| number` | `"400px"` | Maximum height before scrolling. |
| filterable | `boolean` | `true` | Show filter badges by log level. |
| autoScroll | `boolean` | `true` | Auto-scroll to bottom when logs change. |
| title | `string` | -- | Title displayed in the header bar. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

#### LogEntry

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| level | `"info" \| "warn" \| "error" \| "debug"` | `"info"` | Severity level. |
| time | `string` | -- | Timestamp string. |
| message | `string` | -- | Log message text. |

#### Example

```jsx
import { LogViewer } from '@btcv/ui/CodeBlock';

<LogViewer
  title="Server Logs"
  logs={[
    { level: 'info', time: '22:52:50', message: 'Server started' },
    { level: 'warn', time: '22:53:14', message: 'Low disk space' },
    { level: 'error', time: '22:56:01', message: 'Connection timeout' },
  ]}
  maxHeight={400}
  filterable
  autoScroll
/>
```

---

## Collapse

`import { Collapse, CollapseGroup } from '@btcv/ui/Collapse'`

### Collapse

Animated collapsible panel with smooth height transition. Supports controlled, uncontrolled, and accordion modes.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| title | `ReactNode` | **(required)** | Header text displayed in the trigger button. |
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>` | -- | Icon component displayed before the title. |
| defaultOpen | `boolean` | `false` | Whether the collapse is open by default (uncontrolled mode). |
| open | `boolean` | -- | Controlled open state. When provided, the component becomes controlled. |
| onOpenChange | `(open: boolean) => void` | -- | Callback fired when the open state changes (controlled mode). |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<div>` HTML attributes (except `title`).

### CollapseGroup

Layout wrapper that spaces multiple Collapse components vertically. Set `accordion` to enable single-open behavior.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| accordion | `boolean` | `false` | When true, only one Collapse can be open at a time. Opening one closes the others. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<div>` HTML attributes.

### Example

```jsx
{/* Uncontrolled */}
<Collapse title="Details" icon={Info}>
  <p>Hidden content revealed on click.</p>
</Collapse>

{/* Controlled */}
<Collapse title="Settings" open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <p>Controlled collapse content.</p>
</Collapse>

{/* Accordion mode */}
<CollapseGroup accordion>
  <Collapse title="Section 1">Content 1</Collapse>
  <Collapse title="Section 2">Content 2</Collapse>
</CollapseGroup>
```

---

## Typography

`import { PageTitle, PageDescription, SectionTitle, H1, H2, H3, Caption, InlineCode, AppName } from '@btcv/ui/Typography'`

### PageTitle

Page-level heading (h2) with a gold accent bar or icon on the left.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number }>` | -- | Optional Lucide icon. When provided, replaces the default gold accent bar. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<h2>` HTML attributes.

### PageDescription

Muted description text (p) below a PageTitle. Accepts `className` and all `<p>` attributes.

### SectionTitle

Section heading (h3) in uppercase with wide letter spacing. Accepts `className` and all `<h3>` attributes.

### H1

Primary heading (h1, 24px, semibold). Accepts `className` and all `<h1>` attributes.

### H2

Secondary heading (h2, 18px, semibold). Accepts `className` and all `<h2>` attributes.

### H3

Tertiary heading (h3, 14px, semibold). Accepts `className` and all `<h3>` attributes.

### Caption

Small muted text (span, 12px) for secondary information. Accepts `className` and all `<span>` attributes.

### InlineCode

Inline monospace code text (code, 13px). Accepts `className` and all `<code>` attributes.

### AppName

Branded application name with gold suffix.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| name | `string` | **(required)** | Application name (left part, foreground color). |
| suffix | `string` | **(required)** | Application suffix (right part, primary/gold color). |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<span>` HTML attributes.

### Example

```jsx
<PageTitle>Dashboard</PageTitle>
<PageTitle icon={Palette}>Couleurs</PageTitle>
<PageDescription>Overview of system metrics.</PageDescription>
<SectionTitle>Configuration</SectionTitle>
<H1>Dashboard Overview</H1>
<H2>Recent Jobs</H2>
<H3>Job Information</H3>
<Caption>Added 11 minutes ago</Caption>
<InlineCode>598ff1be-f67c-4c79-ab2e-b68021fc12ca</InlineCode>
<AppName name="HANUS" suffix="AIO" />
```

## DarkMode

`import { ThemeToggle, setTheme, toggleTheme } from '@btcv/ui/DarkMode'`

### ThemeToggle

Button that toggles between dark and light theme. Shows Sun icon in dark mode and Moon icon in light mode. Persists the choice in localStorage under `"btcv-theme"`. On mount, reads the stored theme and applies it.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| size | `"sm"` \| `"md"` \| `"lg"` | `"md"` | Size of the toggle button. |
| onChange | `(theme: "dark" \| "light") => void` | -- | Callback fired after the theme changes. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<button>` HTML attributes (except `children` and `onChange`).

### setTheme (function)

Set the theme to `"dark"` or `"light"`. Persists in localStorage and applies to the document. SSR-safe.

```jsx
setTheme("light");
setTheme("dark");
```

### toggleTheme (function)

Toggle between dark and light theme. Returns the new theme (`"dark" | "light"`). SSR-safe.

```jsx
const newTheme = toggleTheme();
```

### Example

```jsx
<Navbar appName="APP" actions={<ThemeToggle />}>
  ...
</Navbar>

<ThemeToggle size="sm" className="fixed bottom-4 right-4" />
```

---

## Widget

`import { StatWidget, ListWidget, ListWidgetLine, CustomWidget, TextWidget } from '@btcv/ui/Widget'`

All widgets are built on Card and share these common optional props:

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| variant | `"primary"` \| `"info"` \| `"success"` \| `"destructive"` \| `"warning"` | -- | Accent color variant. |
| accent | `boolean` | `!!variant` | Show colored top border. Defaults to true when variant is set. |

### StatWidget

Compact widget for displaying a single key metric.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>` | -- | Icon displayed in a colored box beside the stat. |
| title | `ReactNode` | **(required)** | Descriptive label above the value. |
| value | `ReactNode` | **(required)** | Primary metric value displayed prominently. |
| variant | see above | -- | Accent color variant. |
| accent | see above | `!!variant` | Show colored top border. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### ListWidget

Widget displaying a list of items with an optional header. Use `ListWidgetLine` as children.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| title | `ReactNode` | -- | Title displayed in the widget header. |
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>` | -- | Icon displayed beside the title. |
| variant | see above | -- | Accent color variant. |
| accent | see above | `!!variant` | Show colored top border. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### ListWidgetLine

A single line inside a ListWidget.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>` | -- | Icon displayed in a box at the start of the line. |
| iconClassName | `string` | -- | Additional class names for the icon container. |
| imageUrl | `string` | -- | Image URL that replaces the icon when provided. |
| variant | `"primary"` \| `"info"` \| `"success"` \| `"destructive"` \| `"warning"` | -- | Variant controlling the icon color. |
| subtitle | `ReactNode` | -- | Secondary text displayed below the main content. |
| suffix | `ReactNode` | -- | Content displayed on the right side (badge, status, value). |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### CustomWidget

Widget with optional header and free-form children content. Like ListWidget but without the list structure.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| title | `ReactNode` | -- | Title displayed in the widget header. |
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>` | -- | Icon displayed beside the title. |
| variant | see above | -- | Accent color variant. |
| accent | see above | `!!variant` | Show colored top border. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### TextWidget

Widget with header and text content auto-styled as muted description.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| title | `ReactNode` | -- | Title displayed in the widget header. |
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>` | -- | Icon displayed beside the title. |
| variant | see above | -- | Accent color variant. |
| accent | see above | `!!variant` | Show colored top border. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### Example

```jsx
<StatWidget icon={Users} title="Total Users" value="1,234" variant="success" />

<ListWidget title="Activite recente" icon={BarChart3} variant="primary">
  <ListWidgetLine icon={Download} subtitle="il y a 5 min">Fichier telecharge</ListWidgetLine>
  <ListWidgetLine icon={Cpu} subtitle="il y a 12 min">Build termine</ListWidgetLine>
</ListWidget>

<CustomWidget title="Telechargement" icon={Download} variant="primary">
  <ProgressBar value={67} />
</CustomWidget>

<TextWidget title="Info systeme" icon={Shield} variant="info">
  Maintenance prevue demain a 3h.
</TextWidget>
```

---

## Sidebar

`import { SidebarLayout, SidebarTrigger, Sidebar, SidebarInset, SidebarHeader, SidebarContent, SidebarFooter, SidebarGroup, SidebarSeparator, SidebarItem, EasySidebar, useSidebar } from '@btcv/ui/Sidebar'`

### Primitive Components

Build custom sidebar layouts with these composable components.

#### SidebarLayout

Provider and responsive shell. Wraps the entire sidebar + main content area.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| navbarHeight | `number` | `56` | Height offset for a fixed navbar above the sidebar. Controls `top` and `height`. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

#### SidebarTrigger

Hamburger toggle button. Only visible on mobile (`md:hidden`).

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<button>` HTML attributes.

#### Sidebar

The aside panel. Fixed on mobile with slide-in/out animation. Sticky on desktop. Default width: `w-[220px]`.

The panel is a single element that both reserves the layout width and is the visible card, so **width is controlled directly via `className`** — pass `w-*` / `max-w-*` (e.g. `className="w-64"` or `className="w-full max-w-xs"`) and tailwind-merge lets it win over the default. No width prop needed.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| navbarHeight | `number` | `56` | Height offset matching the fixed navbar. |
| variant | `"default"` \| `"floating"` | `"default"` | `"default"`: full-height panel flush against the edge with a right border. `"floating"`: detached card with margin all around, full rounded border and a soft shadow. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. Also the way to set width (`w-*` / `max-w-*`). |

#### SidebarInset

Main content area next to the sidebar.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| navbarHeight | `number` | `56` | Height offset for top padding. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

#### SidebarHeader

Top section of the sidebar. Padded (`px-4 py-4`). Accepts `className`.

#### SidebarContent

Scrollable middle section. Padded (`py-2 px-2`). Accepts `className`.

#### SidebarFooter

Bottom section with top border. Padded (`px-2 py-3`). Accepts `className`.

#### SidebarGroup

Group of sidebar items with an optional label.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| label | `string` | -- | Optional group label displayed above the items. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

#### SidebarSeparator

Horizontal divider line. Accepts `className`.

#### SidebarItem

Navigation item with icon, active state, and badge.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>` | -- | Icon component rendered before the label. |
| active | `boolean` | -- | Whether this item is currently active. |
| badge | `ReactNode` | -- | Optional badge or indicator rendered on the right side. |
| asChild | `boolean` | `false` | Render as the child element (e.g., NavLink, anchor). |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native HTML element attributes. Clicking closes the sidebar on mobile.

#### SidebarSubMenu

Container for indented sub-items. Used after a `SidebarItem` (always-expanded) or inside a `SidebarCollapsible` (auto-managed).

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

#### SidebarSubItem

Indented child item rendered inside a `SidebarSubMenu`. Lighter than `SidebarItem` (no icon, reduced padding).

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| active | `boolean` | -- | Whether this sub-item is currently active. |
| badge | `ReactNode` | -- | Optional badge rendered on the right. |
| asChild | `boolean` | `false` | Render as the child element (e.g., NavLink, anchor). |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

#### SidebarCollapsible

Parent item with a chevron that toggles its sub-items. Auto-opens when any direct child has `active={true}`, unless `defaultOpen` is provided.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; className?: string }>` | -- | Icon rendered before the label. |
| label | `ReactNode` | **(required)** | Trigger label. |
| badge | `ReactNode` | -- | Optional badge between label and chevron. |
| active | `boolean` | -- | Whether the parent itself is in active state. |
| defaultOpen | `boolean` | auto | Initial open state. When omitted, auto-opens if any child is active. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### useSidebar (hook)

Returns `{ open: boolean; setOpen: Dispatch<SetStateAction<boolean>> }` from the SidebarLayout context.

### EasySidebar

Declarative sidebar with items/groups/separators configuration. Built on the primitives above.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| items | `EasySidebarEntry[]` | **(required)** | Sidebar entries: items, groups, or `"separator"` strings. |
| header | `ReactNode` | -- | Header content rendered above the navigation. |
| footer | `ReactNode` | -- | Footer content rendered below the navigation. |
| renderLink | `(item: EasySidebarItem) => ReactElement` | -- | Custom link renderer for router integration (NavLink, Next.js Link, etc.). When provided, SidebarItem renders with `asChild`. |
| onNavigate | `(key: string) => void` | -- | Callback when an item is clicked. Receives the item key. |
| navbarHeight | `number` | `56` | Height offset matching the fixed navbar. |
| variant | `"default"` \| `"floating"` | `"default"` | `"floating"`: detached card with margin all around, full rounded border and a soft shadow. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes, forwarded to the panel. Use `w-*` / `max-w-*` here to set the width. |

**EasySidebarEntry** is one of:
- `EasySidebarItem`: `{ key, label, icon?, href?, badge?, active?, children?, collapsible?, defaultOpen? }`
- `EasySidebarGroup`: `{ label?: string; items: EasySidebarItem[] }`
- `"separator"` (string literal)

When `children` is set on an `EasySidebarItem`:
- Without `collapsible`, sub-items render in a `SidebarSubMenu` always visible below the parent.
- With `collapsible: true`, the parent renders as a `SidebarCollapsible` with a chevron. `defaultOpen` controls initial state; when omitted, auto-opens if any child has `active: true`.

### Example

```jsx
{/* Primitive usage */}
<SidebarLayout>
  <Sidebar>
    <SidebarHeader>
      <AppName name="BATCAVE" suffix="UI" />
    </SidebarHeader>
    <SidebarContent>
      <SidebarGroup label="Navigation">
        <SidebarItem icon={Home} active>Dashboard</SidebarItem>
        <SidebarItem icon={Settings}>Settings</SidebarItem>
      </SidebarGroup>
    </SidebarContent>
  </Sidebar>
  <SidebarInset>
    <main>Content here</main>
  </SidebarInset>
</SidebarLayout>

{/* Declarative usage */}
<SidebarLayout>
  <EasySidebar
    header={<AppName name="BATCAVE" suffix="UI" />}
    items={[
      { label: "Navigation", items: [
        { key: "home", label: "Dashboard", icon: Home, active: true },
        { key: "settings", label: "Settings", icon: Settings },
      ]},
      "separator",
      { key: "logout", label: "Logout", icon: LogOut },
    ]}
    renderLink={(item) => <NavLink to={item.href}>{item.label}</NavLink>}
  />
  <SidebarInset>
    <main>Content here</main>
  </SidebarInset>
</SidebarLayout>
```

---

## Combobox

`import { Combobox } from '@btcv/ui/Combobox'`

Searchable select dropdown combining cmdk for filtering and Radix Popover for positioning. Styled to match the Input component. Selecting the already-selected value deselects it.

### ComboboxOption Type

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| value | `string` | The value submitted when this option is selected. |
| label | `string` | The display label for this option. |
| icon | `React.ComponentType<{ className?: string }>` | Optional icon component rendered before the label. |

### Combobox Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| options | `ComboboxOption[]` | -- | Available options to search and select from. |
| value | `string` | -- | Controlled selected value. |
| onChange | `(value: string) => void` | -- | Callback fired when the selected value changes. |
| placeholder | `string` | `"Rechercher..."` | Placeholder text for the search input. |
| emptyMessage | `string` | `"Aucun resultat."` | Message shown when no options match the search query. |
| disabled | `boolean` | `false` | Whether the combobox is disabled. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the trigger button. |

### Example

```jsx
const [selected, setSelected] = React.useState("");

<Combobox
  options={[
    { value: "docker", label: "Docker" },
    { value: "totanus", label: "Totanus" },
    { value: "k8s", label: "Kubernetes" },
  ]}
  value={selected}
  onChange={setSelected}
  placeholder="Rechercher un service..."
/>
```

---

## DatePicker

`import { DatePicker, Calendar } from '@btcv/ui/DatePicker'`

Date picker input that opens a calendar popover on click. Uses react-day-picker with BTCV styling and Intl.DateTimeFormat (fr-FR) for locale-aware formatting.

### DatePicker Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| value | `Date` | -- | Currently selected date (controlled). |
| onChange | `(date: Date \| undefined) => void` | -- | Callback fired when the selected date changes. |
| placeholder | `string` | `"Choisir une date"` | Placeholder text shown when no date is selected. |
| label | `string` | -- | Optional label text displayed above the trigger. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the trigger button. |

### Calendar Props

Standalone calendar component wrapping react-day-picker. Accepts all `DayPickerProps` from react-day-picker plus:

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the calendar wrapper. |

All standard DayPicker props (`mode`, `selected`, `onSelect`, `disabled`, `fromDate`, `toDate`, etc.) are forwarded directly.

### Example

```jsx
const [date, setDate] = React.useState<Date>();

{/* Full date picker with trigger button */}
<DatePicker label="Date de naissance" value={date} onChange={setDate} />

{/* Standalone calendar */}
<Calendar mode="single" selected={date} onSelect={setDate} />
```

---

## Dropdown

`import { DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuSeparator, DropdownMenuLabel, DropdownMenuGroup } from '@btcv/ui/Dropdown'`

Dropdown menu built on Radix DropdownMenu with BTCV styling and animations.

### DropdownMenu

Root component. Re-export of `@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu` Root. Accepts `open`, `onOpenChange`, `defaultOpen`, `modal`.

### DropdownMenuTrigger

Re-export of Radix DropdownMenu.Trigger. Accepts `asChild`.

### DropdownMenuGroup

Re-export of Radix DropdownMenu.Group. Used to group related items.

### DropdownMenuContent Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| sideOffset | `number` | `4` | Distance in pixels between trigger and content. |
| container | `HTMLElement` | `document.body` | Portal container. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all Radix `DropdownMenu.Content` props (`side`, `align`, `alignOffset`, etc.).

### DropdownMenuItem Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| inset | `boolean` | -- | Add left padding to align with items that have icons. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all Radix `DropdownMenu.Item` props (`disabled`, `onSelect`, `textValue`).

### DropdownMenuSeparator Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### DropdownMenuLabel Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| inset | `boolean` | -- | Add left padding to align with items that have icons. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### Example

```jsx
<DropdownMenu>
  <DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">Options</Button>
  </DropdownMenuTrigger>
  <DropdownMenuContent>
    <DropdownMenuLabel>Actions</DropdownMenuLabel>
    <DropdownMenuSeparator />
    <DropdownMenuGroup>
      <DropdownMenuItem onSelect={() => console.log("edit")}>Edit</DropdownMenuItem>
      <DropdownMenuItem onSelect={() => console.log("delete")}>Delete</DropdownMenuItem>
    </DropdownMenuGroup>
  </DropdownMenuContent>
</DropdownMenu>
```

---

## HoverCard

`import { HoverCard, HoverCardTrigger, HoverCardContent } from '@btcv/ui/HoverCard'`

Card that appears on hover, built on Radix HoverCard with BTCV styling and enter/exit animations.

### HoverCard

Root component. Re-export of `@radix-ui/react-hover-card` Root. Accepts `open`, `onOpenChange`, `openDelay`, `closeDelay`.

### HoverCardTrigger

Re-export of Radix HoverCard.Trigger. Accepts `asChild`.

### HoverCardContent Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| align | `"start"` \| `"center"` \| `"end"` | `"center"` | Horizontal alignment relative to the trigger. |
| sideOffset | `number` | `4` | Distance in pixels between trigger and content. |
| container | `HTMLElement` | `document.body` | Portal container. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all Radix `HoverCard.Content` props (`side`, `alignOffset`, `avoidCollisions`, etc.).

### Example

```jsx
<HoverCard>
  <HoverCardTrigger asChild>
    <a href="/user/profile">@username</a>
  </HoverCardTrigger>
  <HoverCardContent>
    <div className="flex gap-3">
      <img src="/avatar.jpg" className="size-10 rounded-full" />
      <div>
        <p className="text-sm font-semibold">Username</p>
        <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground">Joined March 2024</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </HoverCardContent>
</HoverCard>
```

## Menubar

`import { Menubar, MenubarMenu, MenubarTrigger, MenubarContent, MenubarItem, MenubarSeparator, MenubarLabel, MenubarGroup } from '@btcv/ui/Menubar'`

Horizontal menu bar built on Radix Menubar with BTCV styling. Supports keyboard navigation and submenus.

### Menubar Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the menubar container. |

Also accepts all Radix `Menubar.Root` props.

### MenubarMenu

Re-export of Radix Menubar.Menu. Wraps a single menu (trigger + content).

### MenubarTrigger Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all Radix `Menubar.Trigger` props.

### MenubarContent Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| sideOffset | `number` | `8` | Distance in pixels between trigger and content. |
| alignOffset | `number` | `-4` | Alignment offset in pixels. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all Radix `Menubar.Content` props.

### MenubarItem Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| inset | `boolean` | -- | Add left padding to align with items that have icons. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all Radix `Menubar.Item` props (`disabled`, `onSelect`, `textValue`).

### MenubarSeparator Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### MenubarLabel Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| inset | `boolean` | `false` | Add left indent to align with items that have icons. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### MenubarGroup

Re-export of Radix Menubar.Group. Used to group related items.

### Example

```jsx
<Menubar>
  <MenubarMenu>
    <MenubarTrigger>File</MenubarTrigger>
    <MenubarContent>
      <MenubarItem onSelect={() => console.log("new")}>New</MenubarItem>
      <MenubarItem onSelect={() => console.log("open")}>Open</MenubarItem>
      <MenubarSeparator />
      <MenubarItem onSelect={() => console.log("save")}>Save</MenubarItem>
    </MenubarContent>
  </MenubarMenu>
  <MenubarMenu>
    <MenubarTrigger>Edit</MenubarTrigger>
    <MenubarContent>
      <MenubarItem>Undo</MenubarItem>
      <MenubarItem>Redo</MenubarItem>
    </MenubarContent>
  </MenubarMenu>
</Menubar>
```

## Pagination

`import { Pagination, PaginationContent, PaginationItem, PaginationLink, PaginationPrevious, PaginationNext, PaginationEllipsis } from '@btcv/ui/Pagination'`

Composable pagination controls with no external dependencies beyond lucide-react icons. Uses native buttons (not anchor links).

### Pagination Props

Semantic `<nav>` wrapper with `role="navigation"` and `aria-label="pagination"`.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<nav>` HTML attributes.

### PaginationContent Props

Flex container (`<ul>`) for pagination items.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### PaginationItem Props

List item (`<li>`) wrapper for a single pagination element.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### PaginationLink Props

Individual page button styled as a ghost button with an active state.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| isActive | `boolean` | `false` | Whether this link represents the current page. Applies `bg-primary/15 text-primary` styling. |
| size | `"sm"` \| `"md"` \| `"lg"` | `"md"` | Button size controlling height and width. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<button>` HTML attributes.

### PaginationPrevious Props

"Previous" button with a left chevron icon.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| label | `React.ReactNode` | -- | Optional label text displayed next to the chevron icon. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Inherits all PaginationLink props except `children`.

### PaginationNext Props

"Next" button with a right chevron icon.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| label | `React.ReactNode` | -- | Optional label text displayed next to the chevron icon. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Inherits all PaginationLink props except `children`.

### PaginationEllipsis Props

Ellipsis indicator ("...") for skipped page ranges. Renders a `<span>` with a `MoreHorizontal` icon.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### Example

```jsx
<Pagination>
  <PaginationContent>
    <PaginationItem>
      <PaginationPrevious onClick={goPrev} label="Precedent" />
    </PaginationItem>
    <PaginationItem><PaginationLink isActive>1</PaginationLink></PaginationItem>
    <PaginationItem><PaginationLink>2</PaginationLink></PaginationItem>
    <PaginationItem><PaginationLink>3</PaginationLink></PaginationItem>
    <PaginationItem><PaginationEllipsis /></PaginationItem>
    <PaginationItem><PaginationLink>10</PaginationLink></PaginationItem>
    <PaginationItem>
      <PaginationNext onClick={goNext} label="Suivant" />
    </PaginationItem>
  </PaginationContent>
</Pagination>
```

---

## Popover

`import { Popover, PopoverTrigger, PopoverContent, PopoverClose } from '@btcv/ui/Popover'`

General-purpose popover built on Radix Popover with BTCV styling and enter/exit animations.

### Popover

Root component. Re-export of `@radix-ui/react-popover` Root. Accepts `open`, `onOpenChange`, `defaultOpen`, `modal`.

### PopoverTrigger

Re-export of Radix Popover.Trigger. Accepts `asChild`.

### PopoverClose

Re-export of Radix Popover.Close. Accepts `asChild`.

### PopoverContent Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| align | `"start"` \| `"center"` \| `"end"` | `"center"` | Horizontal alignment relative to the trigger. |
| sideOffset | `number` | `4` | Distance in pixels between trigger and content. |
| container | `HTMLElement` | `document.body` | Portal container. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all Radix `Popover.Content` props (`side`, `alignOffset`, `avoidCollisions`, etc.).

### Example

```jsx
<Popover>
  <PopoverTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">Open popover</Button>
  </PopoverTrigger>
  <PopoverContent className="w-80">
    <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
      <p className="text-sm font-medium">Popover Title</p>
      <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">Some content here.</p>
    </div>
  </PopoverContent>
</Popover>
```

## Resizable

`import { ResizablePanelGroup, ResizablePanel, ResizableHandle } from '@btcv/ui/Resizable'`

Resizable panel layout built on react-resizable-panels with BTCV styling. Supports horizontal and vertical layouts with draggable dividers.

### ResizablePanelGroup Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| direction | `"horizontal"` \| `"vertical"` | -- | Layout direction for the panel group. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the container. |

Also accepts all react-resizable-panels `PanelGroupProps` (`autoSaveId`, `onLayout`, `storage`, etc.).

### ResizablePanel Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| defaultSize | `number` | -- | Initial size as a percentage (0-100). |
| minSize | `number` | -- | Minimum size as a percentage. |
| maxSize | `number` | -- | Maximum size as a percentage. |
| collapsible | `boolean` | -- | Whether the panel can be collapsed to zero width/height. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the panel. |

Also accepts all react-resizable-panels `PanelProps` (`collapsedSize`, `onCollapse`, `onExpand`, `onResize`, etc.).

### ResizableHandle Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| withHandle | `boolean` | `false` | Show a draggable grip icon (GripVertical) on the handle. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the handle. |

Also accepts all react-resizable-panels `PanelResizeHandleProps` (except `children`).

### Example

```jsx
<ResizablePanelGroup direction="horizontal" className="min-h-[300px] rounded-lg border border-border">
  <ResizablePanel defaultSize={50} minSize={30}>
    <div className="p-4">Left panel</div>
  </ResizablePanel>
  <ResizableHandle withHandle />
  <ResizablePanel defaultSize={50} minSize={20}>
    <div className="p-4">Right panel</div>
  </ResizablePanel>
</ResizablePanelGroup>
```

---

## Separator

`import { Separator } from '@btcv/ui/Separator'`

A visual divider between content sections. Supports horizontal and vertical orientation.

### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| orientation | `"horizontal"` \| `"vertical"` | `"horizontal"` | Direction of the separator line. |
| decorative | `boolean` | `true` | When true, sets role="none" instead of role="separator". |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all standard `<div>` HTML attributes.

### Example

```jsx
// Horizontal (default)
<Separator />

// Vertical in a flex container
<div className="flex items-center gap-4">
  <span>Left</span>
  <Separator orientation="vertical" />
  <span>Right</span>
</div>
```

---

## Sheet

`import { Sheet, SheetTrigger, SheetClose, SheetPortal, SheetOverlay, SheetContent, SheetHeader, SheetFooter, SheetTitle, SheetDescription, sheetVariants } from '@btcv/ui/Sheet'`

Slide-in side panel (drawer) built on Radix Dialog with CVA variants for side positioning and CSS transitions.

### Sheet

Root component. Re-export of `@radix-ui/react-dialog` Root. Accepts `open`, `onOpenChange`, `defaultOpen`, `modal`.

### SheetTrigger

Re-export of Radix Dialog.Trigger. Accepts `asChild`.

### SheetClose

Re-export of Radix Dialog.Close. Accepts `asChild`.

### SheetPortal

Re-export of Radix Dialog.Portal.

### SheetOverlay Props

Fixed full-screen backdrop with blur and fade animation.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### SheetContent Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| side | `"top"` \| `"bottom"` \| `"left"` \| `"right"` | `"right"` | Which edge of the screen the sheet slides in from. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Includes a built-in close button (X icon) in the top-right corner. Also accepts all Radix `Dialog.Content` props.

### SheetHeader Props

Flex column layout for the sheet header area.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### SheetFooter Props

Footer area pinned to the bottom with a top border. Uses `mt-auto` to push to the bottom.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### SheetTitle Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all Radix `Dialog.Title` props.

### SheetDescription Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all Radix `Dialog.Description` props.

### sheetVariants

CVA variant generator for sheet positioning. Returns Tailwind classes for a given `side`.

### Example

```jsx
<Sheet>
  <SheetTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">Open Sheet</Button>
  </SheetTrigger>
  <SheetContent side="right">
    <SheetHeader>
      <SheetTitle>Settings</SheetTitle>
      <SheetDescription>Adjust your preferences below.</SheetDescription>
    </SheetHeader>
    <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-5 py-4">
      {/* Sheet body content */}
    </div>
    <SheetFooter>
      <SheetClose asChild>
        <Button variant="secondary">Cancel</Button>
      </SheetClose>
      <Button>Save</Button>
    </SheetFooter>
  </SheetContent>
</Sheet>
```

## Drawer

`import { Drawer, DrawerTrigger, DrawerClose, DrawerContent, DrawerHeader, DrawerFooter, DrawerTitle, DrawerDescription } from '@btcv/ui/Drawer'`

Mobile-friendly bottom sheet built on [vaul](https://github.com/emilkowalski/vaul). Slides up from the bottom of the screen with a drag handle for swipe-to-dismiss. Ideal for mobile navigation, action sheets, and responsive dialogs.

### Drawer

Root component. Wraps vaul's `Drawer.Root`. Sets `shouldScaleBackground` to `true` by default.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| shouldScaleBackground | `boolean` | `true` | Whether the background content scales down when the drawer opens. |
| open | `boolean` | -- | Controlled open state. |
| onOpenChange | `(open: boolean) => void` | -- | Callback when the open state changes. |

Also accepts all vaul `Drawer.Root` props (`modal`, `dismissible`, `snapPoints`, etc.).

### DrawerTrigger

Re-export of vaul's `Drawer.Trigger`. Accepts `asChild`.

### DrawerClose

Re-export of vaul's `Drawer.Close`. Accepts `asChild`.

### DrawerContent Props

Bottom-anchored content panel with overlay, rounded top corners, and a drag handle bar.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Max height is `85dvh`. Includes a centered drag handle indicator at the top. Also accepts all vaul `Drawer.Content` props.

### DrawerHeader Props

Centered flex column layout for the drawer header.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### DrawerFooter Props

Footer area with a top border.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

### DrawerTitle Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all vaul `Drawer.Title` props.

### DrawerDescription Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all vaul `Drawer.Description` props.

### Example

```jsx
<Drawer>
  <DrawerTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">Open Drawer</Button>
  </DrawerTrigger>
  <DrawerContent>
    <DrawerHeader>
      <DrawerTitle>Edit Profile</DrawerTitle>
      <DrawerDescription>Make changes to your profile here.</DrawerDescription>
    </DrawerHeader>
    <div className="px-4 py-3">
      {/* Drawer body content */}
    </div>
    <DrawerFooter>
      <DrawerClose asChild>
        <Button variant="secondary">Cancel</Button>
      </DrawerClose>
      <Button>Save</Button>
    </DrawerFooter>
  </DrawerContent>
</Drawer>
```

---

## Skeleton

`import { Skeleton, SkeletonText, SkeletonCircle } from '@btcv/ui/Skeleton'`

Animated loading placeholders with pulse animation. Three variants for different content types.

### Skeleton Props

Animated pulsing rounded rectangle. Control size via `className` or inline `style`.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. Use width/height utilities (e.g. `h-4 w-48`) to control size. |

Also accepts all native `<div>` HTML attributes.

### SkeletonText Props

Multiple skeleton lines simulating a block of text. The last line renders at 75% width for a natural look.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| lines | `number` | `3` | Number of text lines to render. |
| gap | `string` | `"gap-2"` | Tailwind gap class between lines. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes for the container. |

Also accepts all native `<div>` HTML attributes.

### SkeletonCircle Props

Circular skeleton placeholder for avatar or icon loading states.

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| size | `string` | `"size-10"` | Diameter as a Tailwind size class (e.g. `"size-10"`, `"size-16"`). |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all native `<div>` HTML attributes.

### Example

```jsx
{/* Basic skeleton */}
<Skeleton className="h-4 w-48" />

{/* Text block placeholder */}
<SkeletonText lines={4} />

{/* Avatar placeholder */}
<SkeletonCircle size="size-12" />

{/* Card loading state */}
<div className="flex items-center gap-3">
  <SkeletonCircle />
  <div className="flex-1">
    <SkeletonText lines={2} />
  </div>
</div>
```

---

## Tooltip

`import { TooltipProvider, Tooltip, TooltipTrigger, TooltipContent } from '@btcv/ui/Tooltip'`

Tooltip built on Radix Tooltip with BTCV styling and enter/exit animations. Requires a `TooltipProvider` wrapper (typically at the app root).

### TooltipProvider

Re-export of `@radix-ui/react-tooltip` Provider. Accepts `delayDuration`, `skipDelayDuration`, `disableHoverableContent`.

### Tooltip

Re-export of Radix Tooltip.Root. Accepts `open`, `onOpenChange`, `defaultOpen`, `delayDuration`.

### TooltipTrigger

Re-export of Radix Tooltip.Trigger. Accepts `asChild`.

### TooltipContent Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| sideOffset | `number` | `6` | Distance in pixels between trigger and tooltip. |
| className | `string` | -- | Additional CSS classes. |

Also accepts all Radix `Tooltip.Content` props (`side`, `align`, `alignOffset`, `avoidCollisions`, etc.).

### Example

```jsx
<TooltipProvider>
  <Tooltip>
    <TooltipTrigger asChild>
      <Button variant="ghost" size="icon">
        <Info size={16} />
      </Button>
    </TooltipTrigger>
    <TooltipContent side="bottom">
      This is a helpful tooltip
    </TooltipContent>
  </Tooltip>
</TooltipProvider>
```
